context.projectOverview: Sprint 2 implements EvaluatorPanelReconciler — a PURE majority-vote reducer over EvalResult[] producing one canonical EvalResult (evaluator='panel'). This is the conformance-critical core (ADR-4). The caller injects the timestamp (no Date.now in the reducer). The JS port + drift gate come in sprint 3.

completedSprints.title: Engine-selection seam + pipeline.engine config
completedSprints.summary: Added pipeline.engine config, PipelineEngine interface + selector under src/orchestrator/workflow/, TS engine wraps the former runPipeline body (now runTsPipeline).

principles: See .bober/principles.md — TS strict, ESLint zero-error, ESM .js specifiers, consistent-type-imports, no synchronous fs, collocated *.test.ts, conventional commits.
notes: EvalResultSchema is at src/contracts/eval-result.ts:60. Reuse the FROZEN EvalResult/EvalDetail types — do not redefine them. The reducer MUST be pure: no Date.now, no new Date(), no Math.random, no fs. Timestamp is a function argument. New files go under src/orchestrator/workflow/ (reconciler.ts + reconciler.test.ts). ADR-4: evaluator='panel', strict majority (passCount > failCount), fail-closed on tie, union failing details. Sprint 3 will hand-write a JS port that must match this exactly — keep the logic simple and explicit so it ports cleanly.
